title: How Can You Make A Real Difference In Medical Aesthetics？
filename: How Can You Make A Real Difference In Medical Aesthetics？.en.vtt
excerpt: Hello everyone I had such a great week last night last week clinically and I've been meaning to do this video since I had two really memorable patients and one of them has gave me permission to share her story which I thought I would do with the hope that I can help everyone else…

segments.text: hello everyone I had such a great week last night last week clinically and I've been meaning to do this video since I had two really memorable patients and one of them has gave me permission to share her story which I thought I would do with the hope that I can help everyone else also do more of this really simple technique which kind of guarantees that you're really going to make a difference to your patients so what more could you ask for I there is nothing that will be better for business and there is nothing that's better for your of lomas for your soul really than to feel like you're using your time on this planet to make a difference to people and there's a really simple technique which I used on both of them which and I've used it a lot of times before which really makes people get the real benefit from their treatment but it starts with understanding first of all that the presenting complaint is not the problem so now and both a case I'm going

to tell you about is really interesting because it really illustrates this well now I actually happen to believe that the presented complaint is nearly always the tip of the iceberg even for really simple things so you know a young girl who wants her lip treated it's a little bit fuller to make it symmetrical it's pretty simple it's so technically easy that often will jump in and go straight to doing it and without digging a little bit deeper about how this is really how she is anticipating this is going to change your life and that's where the where it gets real interesting and that's why I'll start with my story which is someone who presented with a dip in her nose and which sounds and you know it was actually technically a little bit challenging for the reason that she had the dip but um but the most important thing is it sounded simple and when you got talking about what the problem is it became a lot more interesting and Anna said I have asked specifically for her permission to share the story and but the reason that she had a dip in it in her nose was that she had a cocaine addiction which she had thankfully

seemingly got control of at the moment and she hadn't used cocaine for a long period of time but she had this dip on her nose so now the dip on the No the cocaine addiction is obviously understanding and we all none of us want a dip on our nose that wasn't there before but the really interesting thing is which I which the point I'm really trying to make is what why was this obsessing for her now that actually this dip had actually driven her into almost agra phobia which is she would not go out in public at all but she had a sort of hierarchy of places that she could go and couldn't go and for her the worst possible place to go was where other mums were so that's kind of you know the school run was her worst nightmare in fact as we got talking about the different parts of her day that would and that could make a difference that the treatment could make a difference to the school run was ultimately the thing she wanted the most and to be able to do but it may have been too big of a step for her to go straight into but when someone first

presents and they say oh I did my nose if you keep asking why that's troubling them and when it's troubling them particularly when so when is this problem when does it exert an emotional drag on your day is quite a good question because not always that it that it's on their mind but it just kind of it's in a slightly unpleasant moment when they think of it now for her it was way beyond unpleasant it was awful and it was anytime there was another mum around and the reason for that was because they know the dip on her nose represented to her shame and about what she'd done her own flaws and the way I came to understand it was it's a little bit like walking around with all your vulnerability exposed all the time like your worst your deepest darkest secret on display for everyone to see and that's obviously massively difficult to live your life like that so the story was much is much more significant than her nose and than the actual aesthetics so the aesthetics actually probably wasn't as far as cocaine abuse goes you've seen way worse it was it wasn't as significant as in

aesthetically as it might have been to the way it was calling back her life but you've got to understand that all people who are aging you know Heather's a slightly asymmetrical you know feature or a small top lip or something for them it's much deeper than just the aesthetics we know we have to make sure things fit in as dick rule because I was you shouldn't do the treatment if you're making something different but it's not better your aesthetically not helping them but really what we're looking for is to understand where their story would better and this is where I got to with this lady which is if I could change her nose what could she then do differently in her life that's a really important question because therein lies the proof that you were doing you're going to make her life better and so she we talked about different things it and as I said the ultimate thing she wanted was to be able to go on the school run but she ain't done that in four months or something like that so be quite hard for her to go straight to that so I said what else are you not doing that you would like to be able to do and if we came up with something

less threatening which is just to go out for dinner so she had a nice Italian restaurant that she used to go to and she wanted to have to go out to that restaurant again and so that's what I said it also I once I've analyzed her nose and decided a safe way of doing it because by the way like if you've had cocaine damage to your nose you've got a decrease in blood flow to mucous membranes in particular so you've got to do this procedure without affecting tissues that are too closely aligned to mucous membranes so that that's just a technical point where thing about and but the difference the difference I wanted to make the difference for her was that she could go out to dinner so I knew I could create a technical difference and at that point this is the most important thing I say to you before you do the procedure when you've understood someone's story and you can see where life where their life is being held back and you know the specific moment where if you can create a change their life will become different ask them to commit to making that change before you do the procedure so this is what I Center said to her is your

well-being is ultimately the only outcome the matters I shouldn't do this procedure unless I improve your well-being you've told me that your well-being will be improved if you can do something simple like go out for dinner so if I can deliver on the aesthetic result will you make a commitment to me now to actually go out for dinner and patients often really surprised to hear you say this but they were also always happy to hear you say it because it's all that they're almost wanting to do they know that's what they want to do and we all have this ability to kind of focus on the problem in the hope that we will one day be able to meet the solution but we don't always make a plan so you get your patient to make a plan to do the thing that they are hoping they're gonna be able to do if you if they correct their nose but whatever the issue is but you but they haven't often said yes to someone I commit to do that and it's a beautiful thing because they will nine out of ten times they will do it and they actually often may not do it if you haven't asked them to admit to

it so ask for that commitment and you should be able to see then that their life actually changed and this is exactly what's happened in this case so I hope that can but I tell you what you'll get as a practitioner is when they come back for their follow-up and they say I actually did go for dinner and I did do that thing which I've been dreading doing and it was great and I pulled it off you will feel amazing and I know a lot of practitioners have kind of doubts over you know we've all got that old uncle who thinks that aesthetics is superficial and isn't really you know something that you should be doing with your qualification all that can go away for you when you know that you're rewriting people's lives you're essentially helping them rewrite a better story for themselves and you're actually the catalyst that gets things back on track for people so and so that's how you make a difference medicals ethics so actually don't just go and make the aesthetic difference ask for them to commit to making the action that they are hoping is they're gonna be

able to do like some people it's as simple as I'll take a selfie more often or I'll go on Instagram or you know whatever I'll go into their dating whatever it is and but ask them to me to commit to it and you will hopefully get a lot more reward and while you do your job and you will really see the difference you making people's lives and it's really fun it's incredibly rewarding and I hope that helps basically so and if you think that's been useful be great to know if you've got any stories of your own that you can anonymize and be great to share them and because it'd be great to hear from other people who are who already doing I know loads of practitioners already this is the best part of our jobs isn't it when you actually see someone's life improved so if you've got a good story and you can make it change a few details it's not recognizable by the patient and be great to share that and you could tag anyone who's interested in this or share it if you think it's worthwhile but that's my kind of consultation tip for enjoying your practice more making more of a difference and making sure your treatments do what they are really

supposed to do which is improve well-being so help that hope that works I've just noticed because the way I'm doing it I haven't seen the people waving but hi Julie Karen Louise and thanks for your comments hi Lauren so yeah let me know you think and tag someone share it really appreciate your comments and thank you all very much for watching see you again next time

Able to do like some people it's as simple as I'll take a selfie more often or I'll go on Instagram or you know whatever I'll go into their dating whatever it is and but ask them to me to commit to it and you will hopefu…
